animeExpeditionsCodes.title: Anime Expeditions Codes
animeExpeditionsCodes.subtitle: Redeem the latest codes for free Gems and Trait Crystals. Enter each code exactly as displayed.
animeExpeditionsCodes.intro: Open Anime Expeditions, select the three-dot menu in the upper-left corner and choose Redeem Codes. Paste a working code into the Enter Code field, then press Redeem Code to collect the rewards.

animeExpeditionsBeginnerGuide.title: Anime Expeditions Beginner Guide
animeExpeditionsBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Build a usable starting team, clear early Story stages and avoid wasting Gems, Trait Crystals or upgrade currency.
animeExpeditionsBeginnerGuide.intro: Anime Expeditions combines unit summoning with wave-based tower defense. Your early goal is to obtain a balanced roster, place an affordable opening unit and invest most of your battle currency into one reliable damage dealer.

steps.title: Redeem the Launch Codes
steps.description: Redeem EA+, EA and RELEASE from the three-dot menu before summoning. The rewards provide up to 7,500 Gems and 80 Trait Crystals when every access requirement is met.
steps.tip: Save Trait Crystals until you own a unit that will remain in your main team.

steps.title: Complete the Beginner Path
steps.description: Follow the True Saint Beginner Path objectives and collect every available beginner reward. These quests introduce summoning, unit management and the main progression systems.
steps.tip: Claim each completed objective immediately so its tickets and resources can be used during the next step.

steps.title: Use the Beginner Banner
steps.description: Spend Beginner Banner tickets before committing large amounts of Gems to other banners. Build a roster containing an inexpensive opener, an area-damage unit, a boss damage dealer and either control or support.
steps.tip: Use the Rates button and watch the Legendary and Mythic pity counters before every large summon session.

steps.title: Start Story Mode
steps.description: Place your cheapest dependable unit near a path section where it can attack enemies for a long time. Add a second unit only when the first placement cannot safely handle the incoming wave.
steps.tip: Corners, bends and overlapping lanes usually provide better coverage than placing directly beside the entrance.

steps.title: Upgrade Before Overplacing
steps.description: Concentrate early battle currency on the unit producing most of your damage. Add control, support and extra coverage after your main carry can defeat normal waves without leaking enemies.
steps.tip: Several low-upgrade units are often weaker than one properly upgraded carry supported by a slow or damage buff.

steps.title: Level and Evolve Your Core Team
steps.description: Use Story, Expeditions and other available modes to obtain account resources, unit experience and evolution materials. Invest first in units that appear in most of your successful teams.
steps.tip: Do not spend rare evolution or reroll materials on temporary units that you plan to replace after the next banner.

animeExpeditionsTierList.title: Anime Expeditions Unit Tier List
animeExpeditionsTierList.subtitle: A role-based ranking for Story, Expeditions, Raids and general progression. Team utility, upgrade value and mode coverage matter more than rarity alone.
animeExpeditionsTierList.intro: Cursed Student provides the strongest disclosed scaling damage kit, while Salmon Sorcerer and The Hero fill important control and support roles. Other Mythic, Secret and Exclusive units should be selected according to their attack coverage, evolution and team synergy.

units.name: Cursed Student (True Love)
units.reason: Every third regular attack triggers a copied follow-up attack. Bleed stacks inside its range can also create up to three Bat Spirits that perform additional attacks.

units.name: Salmon Sorcerer (Grade 1)
units.reason: Applies a 20% Slow through its upgrades and can Stagger nearby enemies for two seconds when Plummet activates.

units.name: The Hero
units.reason: Hero Blessing increases the damage of Physical units within range, with the buff increasing by another 5% at each upgrade.

animeExpeditionsUnitsAndSummon.title: Anime Expeditions Units and Summon Guide
animeExpeditionsUnitsAndSummon.subtitle: Learn how the Beginner, Standard and Mini banners work, then browse the known roster by rarity and combat role.
animeExpeditionsUnitsAndSummon.intro: Gems are the main summoning currency, while Beginner Path quests provide tickets for the starter banner. The summon screen supports single and 10x pulls, displays current drop rates and tracks separate Legendary and Mythic pity goals.

groups.name: Beginner Banner
groups.detail: Complete the True Saint Beginner Path to earn tickets. The featured pool includes Puppet, String Demon, Hollow, Lady Giant, Elf Mage, Flame Emperor, Salmon Sorcerer and Cursed Student.

groups.name: Standard Banner
groups.detail: Use this banner for general roster building and long-term pity progress. Open the Rates panel before summoning to view the current unit pool and drop percentages.

groups.name: Mini Banner
groups.detail: Mini Banners spotlight a smaller selection of units and are best used when the featured Mythic fills a missing role in your team.

groups.name: Summon Pity
groups.meta: Legendary pity 20 summons / Mythic pity 50 summons
groups.detail: The summon interface shows both pity counters beside the active banner and supports Summon 1x and Summon 10x. A qualifying high-rarity pull completes the matching pity cycle.

units.name: True Saint (Holy)
units.role: Beginner Path and exclusive progression unit

animeExpeditionsTraits.title: Anime Expeditions Traits and Rerolls
animeExpeditionsTraits.subtitle: Compare the strongest damage, speed, range, critical and placement bonuses. Save Trait Crystals for permanent Mythic and Secret units.
animeExpeditionsTraits.intro: Traits are permanent passive bonuses rolled through the Trait station in the Upgrades area. Autoroll can repeat rolls automatically, while the trait history records recent results. Prioritize your main damage carry and stop on a strong high-tier trait instead of spending every Trait Crystal chasing Unbound.

traits.bestFor: A single high-investment boss carry with strong upgrades and abilities
traits.advice: Keep immediately. Chase only on a unit that remains effective with one placement.

traits.bestFor: General DPS units, magic attackers and long-range carries
traits.advice: An excellent stopping point for Story, Expeditions and Raids.

traits.bestFor: Fast attackers, multi-hit units and high-damage boss killers
traits.advice: Keep on critical-focused carries unless specifically chasing Unbound.

traits.bestFor: Damage-over-time units and expensive early placements
traits.advice: Keep on burn or bleed units; reroll on pure attackers only when better options are affordable.

animeExpeditionsEvolution.title: Anime Expeditions Evolution Guide
animeExpeditionsEvolution.subtitle: Evolution changes a unit's form and unlocks stronger attacks or abilities. Build one dependable carry before spreading materials across the roster.
animeExpeditionsEvolution.intro: Use the Evolution station in the Upgrades area to inspect each unit's level requirement, evolution items and Yen cost. Evolution recipes are unit-specific, so select the unit first and farm the materials displayed in its recipe rather than collecting unrelated drops.

stages.role: Secret boss DPS and summon-based carry
stages.requirements: Required unit level, Shadow-specific evolution materials and the Yen cost displayed at the Evolution station
stages.result: Higher damage, stronger summoned attackers and an expanded evolved ability set
stages.priority: Evolve first when Shadow is the main boss killer in Story and Raid teams.

stages.role: Melee area damage and lane control
stages.requirements: Required unit level, Lady Giant evolution materials and the Yen cost displayed at the Evolution station
stages.result: Improved area coverage, stronger upgrades and better performance against grouped enemies
stages.priority: Evolve early when the team lacks reliable crowd clearing.

stages.role: Covers the damage type or range missing from the first evolved unit
stages.requirements: Complete the displayed recipe without using materials reserved for the primary carry
stages.result: A balanced team capable of handling both enemy waves and high-health bosses
stages.priority: Choose ranged damage if the first evolution is melee, or boss damage if the first evolution is mainly AoE.

stages.role: Buffing, control, farming or team utility
stages.requirements: Required level, evolution materials and currency after the core damage team is complete
stages.result: Faster clears, stronger carry damage or improved resource generation
stages.priority: Evolve after two dependable damage units can clear the target content consistently.

animeExpeditionsStoryRaids.title: Anime Expeditions Story, Expeditions and Raids
animeExpeditionsStoryRaids.subtitle: Start with Story to build your account, enter Expeditions for objective-based missions and prepare a specialized team before farming Raids.
animeExpeditionsStoryRaids.intro: Each mode rewards a different kind of team. Story favors balanced and affordable units, Expeditions require players to protect an objective while managing resources, and Raids demand concentrated boss damage supported by crowd control and early-wave coverage.

modes.unlock: Available at the beginning of account progression
modes.objective: Defend the route through enemy waves, complete each act and meet the displayed star conditions.
modes.rewards: Gems, Yen or Gold, player EXP, unit EXP, star rewards, Trait Rerolls and evolution materials
modes.teamPlan: Use one cheap opening unit, one AoE attacker and one boss-focused carry. Place units where paths overlap and upgrade the unit that answers the first dangerous wave.

modes.objective: Gather resources, build the required base structures and complete a rotating Payload objective such as escorting, defending or delivering.
modes.rewards: Progression materials, equipment-related resources, currency and objective completion rewards
modes.teamPlan: Assign one player to early defense and another to objective calls. Bring cheap coverage, wide AoE, control near the Payload and a scaling unit for the final threat.

modes.objective: Survive the opening waves, clear supporting enemies and defeat a powerful boss before the timer expires.
modes.rewards: Evolution materials, Spirit Tokens, character drops, Gems, Yen or Gold and Trait Rerolls
modes.teamPlan: Bring a dedicated boss carry, an AoE unit for adds, a buffer or debuffer and enough cheap defense to survive before the main carry is upgraded.

animeExpeditionsFarming.title: Anime Expeditions Gems, Yen and Time Chamber Farming
animeExpeditionsFarming.subtitle: Use first-clear rewards and quests for high-value currency, then switch to repeatable stages or the Time Chamber based on your current upgrade goal.
animeExpeditionsFarming.intro: Active play provides the fastest account progression because Story stars, quests, Expeditions and Raids award targeted resources. The Time Chamber is best used between active sessions to collect passive rewards without replacing limited daily, weekly or first-clear bonuses.

resources.usedFor: Summoning units and reaching banner pity
resources.bestSources: Story first clears, star objectives, daily and weekly quests, achievements, Beginner Path, Battle Pass and events
resources.activeFarm: Complete unclaimed stars and quests first, then repeat the fastest stage that provides useful secondary rewards.
resources.spendingRule: Save Gems for a defined banner target instead of using isolated pulls across several banners.

resources.usedFor: Unit progression, evolution costs and other account upgrades
resources.bestSources: Story clears, quests, mode rewards, Expeditions and passive Time Chamber income
resources.activeFarm: Repeat a short stage that clears consistently and also levels the units used in the main team.
resources.spendingRule: Upgrade the small group of units used in most teams before investing in collection units.

resources.bestSources: Story stars, daily and weekly quests, Raid rewards, Battle Pass milestones, events and redemption rewards
resources.activeFarm: Prioritize objectives that directly display Trait Crystal or reroll rewards.
resources.spendingRule: Use rerolls on permanent Mythic and Secret carries; keep Primordial, Forsaken or a role-matched Draconic roll.

resources.usedFor: Unlocking evolved forms and stronger abilities
resources.bestSources: Story chapters, Expeditions, Raids, crafting and mode-specific reward shops
resources.activeFarm: Select the unit at the Evolution station, identify its recipe and farm only the stage or mode containing that material.
resources.spendingRule: Finish the main damage carry before dividing rare materials between several units.

resources.usedFor: Raising unit levels, account level and mode unlocks
resources.bestSources: Story progression, repeatable stages, quests, EXP rewards and the Time Chamber
resources.activeFarm: Push new Story acts until the team reaches a wall, then repeat the fastest stable clear.
resources.spendingRule: Keep the active Story and Raid team near the level cap before leveling unused units.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Anime Expeditions

questions.question: What is Anime Expeditions?
questions.answer: Anime Expeditions is a Roblox anime tower defense game developed by Expeditions Entertainment. You summon anime-inspired units, evolve their abilities, and defend against enemy waves and bosses across Story, Expeditions and Raid modes.

questions.question: How do I redeem codes in Anime Expeditions?
questions.answer: Open Anime Expeditions, tap the three-dot menu in the upper-left corner and choose Redeem Codes. Paste a working code into the Enter Code field and press Redeem Code to collect free Gems and Trait Crystals.

questions.question: What are the best units in Anime Expeditions?
questions.answer: Cursed Student (True Love) is the strongest disclosed scaling carry, Salmon Sorcerer (Grade 1) provides top-tier control, and The Hero is a strong physical support. Secret and Exclusive units like Shadow (Divine) and True Saint (Holy) are endgame targets.

questions.question: How does summoning pity work in Anime Expeditions?
questions.answer: The summon screen tracks a Legendary pity at 20 summons and a Mythic pity at 50 summons. A qualifying high-rarity pull completes the matching pity cycle, and you can summon 1x or 10x at a time.

questions.question: What are Traits in Anime Expeditions?
questions.answer: Traits are permanent passive bonuses rolled at the Trait station in the Upgrades area. Unbound, Primordial, Forsaken and Draconic are the strongest options, and you should save Trait Crystals for permanent Mythic and Secret carries.

questions.question: How do I evolve units in Anime Expeditions?
questions.answer: Use the Evolution station in the Upgrades area to inspect each unit's level requirement, evolution items and Yen cost. Recipes are unit-specific, so select the unit first and farm only the materials shown in its recipe.

questions.question: What is the Time Chamber in Anime Expeditions?
questions.answer: The Time Chamber collects passive rewards between active sessions. It supplements, but does not replace, limited daily, weekly and first-clear bonuses from Story, quests, Expeditions and Raids.

questions.question: Is Anime Expeditions free to play?
questions.answer: Yes. Anime Expeditions is free to play on Roblox. Optional summons and progression are accelerated through codes, quests, the Battle Pass and events.
